formations_blocks: Portugal attacking on the front foot
adjustments: Portugal exploiting wide delivery, especially through Nuno Mendes and Cancelo
Portugal using short corners and quick restarts to maintain pressure
key_matchups: João Cancelo vs Uzbekistan wide defenders
Bruno Fernandes on set pieces vs Uzbekistan goalkeeper Nematov
phases.summary: Portugal started the phase brighter and quickly established territorial control, with wide runs and repeated crosses putting Uzbekistan under pressure. Uzbekistan appeared to set up with numbers behind the ball, but they struggled to get a foothold as Portugal kept them pinned back, culminating in Ronaldo’s 7th-minute opener from an early attacking spell.

formations_blocks: Portugal pressing high after the restart
Uzbekistan attempting to recover with set-piece attacks
adjustments: Portugal using repeated free-kick routines around the edge of the box
Fernandes taking a free kick short to Mendes on one occasion
Uzbekistan's discipline deteriorating, conceding multiple fouls
Hydration break interrupting the tempo mid-half
key_matchups: Mendes vs Uzbekistan goalkeeper on the 17' goal
Neto vs Hamrobekov leading to the 15' booking
Cancelo under pressure in the build-up to the disallowed 29' goal
phases.summary: Portugal controlled the phase with aggressive pressing and a heavy emphasis on winning free kicks around the edge of the box. Their set-piece pressure produced goals and constant danger, while Uzbekistan were repeatedly penalized and struggled to stay disciplined, though they briefly thought they had found a way back before VAR ruled the goal out. Portugal then restored their two-goal cushion soon after, keeping the match firmly on their terms.

formations_blocks: Uzbekistan attempting to get back into the game
adjustments: Uzbekistan brought on Alizhonov and Mozgovoy
Portugal brought on Semedo and Neto for Cancelo and Conceição
phases.summary: Portugal go into halftime 3-0 up and the commentary makes clear they have total control of the match. The only tactical information provided for the restart is the double substitution on both sides, as Uzbekistan look for a way back while Portugal refresh their team without any further shape details given.

formations_blocks: Portugal in control after Uzbekistan spells of pressure
Uzbekistan pushing forward despite the scoreline
adjustments: Uzbekistan increased their attacking intent after the break
Portugal regained control after allowing some early second-half pressure
Portugal used set pieces and free kicks to keep Uzbekistan pinned back
key_matchups: Cancelo vs Uzbekistan attack on the disallowed goal
phases.summary: The phase opens with Uzbekistan showing more urgency, but their attacking moments are repeatedly wasted, allowing Portugal to reassert control. Portugal create danger through free kicks and a Ronaldo-led move, then extend the lead to 4-0 via Khusanov’s own goal, while Diogo Costa remains untroubled apart from handling a corner and Fayzullaev’s shot. The overall pattern is a brief Uzbekistan push followed by Portugal’s control through territory, set pieces, and superior chance quality.

formations_blocks: Portugal in possession, controlling the final phase
Uzbekistan in a deeper defensive shape / trying to absorb pressure and counter
adjustments: Portugal used a short-corner routine at 84'
89' cross targeted Ronaldo before the deflection fell to Rafa Leão
key_matchups: Ronaldo vs Uzbekistan defender on the 89' cross
Diogo Costa vs Uzbekistan's late free kick
phases.summary: Portugal spent the closing stages on the ball, managing the tempo after a lull while Uzbekistan tried to break out and counter. Portugal’s substitutions kept the attacking pressure fresh, and although they remained a little loose defensively at times, the late goal and near-sixth showed their control of the phase. Uzbekistan did create a few openings, but their lack of quality in transition and from the set piece at 90+3 meant they could not threaten a comeback.
